title: Question 1.7
statement_text: Let $t\geq 6$ be an integer. Does there exist a graph $G$ with $E(\overline{G})\neq\varnothing$ such that $G$ is $H$-free but $G+e$ has an induced subgraph isomorphic to $C_{2t-2}$ for every $e\in E(\overline{G})$?
context_text: The authors prove Theorem 1.6 constructing graphs that are $C_{2t}$-free but where removing any edge creates an induced copy of $C_{2t}$ (half of the induced saturation condition). Question 1.7 asks about the other half: whether the edge-addition condition can also be satisfied, which would be a key step toward solving the induced saturation problem for all even cycles.
paper_title: Halfway to induced saturation for even cycles

title: Question 1.8
statement_text: Does there exist a polynomial $f$ such that for every $t\geq 3$, there is a graph $G$ on at most $f(t)$ vertices with $E(G)\neq\varnothing$, such that $G$ is $H$-free but $G-e$ has an induced subgraph isomorphic to $C_{2t-2}$ for every $e\in E(G)$?
context_text: The construction of $G_t$ in Theorem 1.6 has doubly exponential size in $t$. The authors remark they do not even have a rationale for expecting the number of vertices to be more than linear in $t$, making this a natural question about the efficiency of the construction.
paper_title: Halfway to induced saturation for even cycles
